operating_state: Nike delivered revenue growth of 16% currency-neutral in Q4 2021 and 8% for six months to $23.6 billion, driven by North America and EMEA strength offset by Greater China and APLA declines. NIKE Direct revenues grew with digital commerce increasing 17% currency-neutral, while wholesale revenues expanded 17% in Q4. Factory closures in Vietnam and Indonesia eliminated three months of production capacity, creating inventory shortages that disproportionately impacted regions with shorter transit times.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 230 basis points to 46.2% for six months, driven by reduced promotional activity due to inventory constraints, higher full-price sales mix, and increased direct-to-consumer margins. Higher freight and logistics costs from supply chain disruptions partially offset margin gains. Selling and administrative expenses increased from elevated demand creation spending, strategic technology investments, wage increases and technology overhead.
material_events: Nike substantially completed Consumer Direct Acceleration organizational restructuring with minimal termination costs versus prior year. The company resumed share repurchases executing $1.7 billion in buybacks during first six months under the four-year $15 billion program. Nike closed its Brazil transaction transitioning to distributor model while Argentina, Chile and Uruguay entities remain held-for-sale.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 factory shutdowns in Vietnam and Indonesia eliminated three months of production with all factories reopening in October but requiring months to return to pre-closure volumes. Extended inventory transit times from port congestion, transportation delays and container shortages created elevated logistics costs and supply chain constraints. New consumer data privacy laws in China create uncertainty regarding interpretation and compliance requirements.
forward_outlook: Management expects inventory supply to significantly lag consumer demand for remainder of fiscal year due to factory closures and elevated transit times. Product costs are expected to increase in second half from higher input costs while transportation and logistics costs continue rising. The company maintains multi-year investment plans for digital transformation and Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy toward fiscal 2025 financial goals.
uncertainty_profile: Additional factory closures or employee absences from COVID-19 variants could further disrupt planned inventory production beyond current three-month loss. New data privacy regulations in China create interpretation uncertainty that may impair operating plan execution. Volatile effective tax rates driven by earnings mix changes across jurisdictions and ongoing supply chain disruptions make quarterly performance comparisons challenging.
